title: aegilemma peregrina subsp. cylindrostachys
intro: Aegilemma peregrina subsp. cylindrostachys, also known as the Yellow Form of the Bitter Apple, is a plant species that belongs to the family Achariaceae. It is a shrub or small tree native to tropical Africa, used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting infections. Aegilemma peregrina subsp. cylindrostachys can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for medicinal purposes, and has been used in traditional remedies in various parts of Africa.
